# Sarmal

> Parametric curve renderer for loading indicators. Beautiful spinners from two lines of math.

Sarmal is a tiny, zero-dependency JavaScript library that renders animated
loading indicators by tracing parametric curves on an HTML canvas element.

## Quick start

### CDN (one line)

```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/@sarmal/core/dist/auto-init.js"></script>
<canvas data-sarmal="artemis2" data-trail-color="#60a5fa"></canvas>
```

### npm

```sh
npm install @sarmal/core
```

```js
import { createSarmal, curves } from '@sarmal/core'

const canvas = document.querySelector('canvas')
const sarmal = createSarmal(canvas, curves.artemis2, {
  trailColor: '#60a5fa',
})
sarmal.start()
```

## Available curves

- `artemis2`: Artemis II free-return lunar trajectory approximation
- `epitrochoid7`: 7-lobed epitrochoid with a breathing distance parameter
- `astroid`: 4-cusped hypocycloid (x=cos³t, y=sin³t)
- `deltoid`: 3-cusped hypocycloid
- `rose5`: 5-petal rose curve (r=cos(5t))
- `rose3`: 3-petal rose curve (r=cos(3t))

## API

### createSarmal(canvas, curveDef, options?)

Returns an instance with `.start()`, `.stop()`, `.destroy()`.

**Options:**
- `trailColor`: CSS color for the animated trail
- `skeletonColor`: CSS color for the static skeleton outline
- `headColor`: CSS color for the leading dot
- `headRadius`: radius of the leading dot (pixels)
- `glowSize`: blur radius for glow effect (pixels)
- `trailLength`: number of points in the trail buffer

The `data-*` attributes on `<canvas>` mirror all options for CDN/no-build usage.

## Links

- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/@sarmal/core
- GitHub: https://github.com/yethranayeh/sarmal
- Site: https://sarmal.art
